commit.message:
Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct api test mocks for jsdom environment and flexible header matching

Use jsdom's native window instead of stubbing globalThis, and match headers
with objectContaining to avoid brittleness from additional default headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui): topbar — injection correcte sur toutes les pages

send_file() utilise direct_passthrough=True → get_data() retournait ''
→ l'injection CSS/JS ne se faisait pas sur les pages HTML.

Fix : pages.py utilise make_response() (lecture en mémoire) au lieu de
send_file(), garantissant que after_request peut modifier le HTML.
Aussi : direct_passthrough=False + garde "if not html" dans le hook
pour robustesse sur toute autre route send_file() éventuelle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix video autoplay: set autoplay attr and play on canplay event

Videos weren't autoplaying because play() was called before any data
loaded (readyState 0). Now sets autoplay attribute when loading and
uses canplay event to trigger play once data is buffered.

commit.message:
Update Docker & Network benchmark results with pre-warmed Daytona boot

Docker: Daytona stock boot 0.28s (was 1.1s) using CreateSandboxBaseParams,
now tied with E2B (6.2s vs 6.1s total). Modal 1.81x custom image speedup.

Network: Daytona leads sustained download (90 MB/s), upload (16.65 MB/s),
DNS (2.37ms), pip install (0.82s). E2B fastest total (8.8s).

docs: update CHAT-003 with run 3 training results (KAIZEN-094)

Run 3 (bias fix + LR=5e-5): epoch 1 loss=1.52 (meaningful learning),
epoch 2 loss=3.88 (catastrophic forgetting). Key insight: full
fine-tuning on 17K samples over-trains after 1 epoch.

Two entrenar bugs fixed upstream:
- #258: missing QKV attention biases (24bc0c7)
- #259: config.json hardcoded LlamaForCausalLM (533358f)

Evaluation pending run 3 completion.

Fix: Auto-create user record on login + Add ensureUserExists method

Critical fix for moments not saving:
- Added ensureUserExists() method to SupabaseAPIClient
- Automatically creates/upserts user record in public.users when logging in
- Previous issue: Auth records existed but no matching database user record
  → Foreign key constraint prevented moment inserts

Changes:
- SupabaseAPIClient: New ensureUserExists(userId, email) method
- AuthManager: Calls ensureUserExists() after successful login
- Adds UserPayload struct for user creation endpoint

This fixes the foreign key constraint violation that was preventing
all moments from being saved to the database.

commit.message:
Unify temp file prefix from webact-rs- to webact-

Shares Chrome profile, session files, screenshots, PDFs, and all other
temp files with the JS version so logins persist across both.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Debounce CLI disconnect notification to eliminate WS cycling effects

CLI cycles its WebSocket every ~30s (close code 1000). Previously,
handleCLIClose immediately broadcast cli_disconnected to browsers and
nulled cliSocket, causing: (a) browser UI flapping between connected/
disconnected states, (b) handleBrowserOpen seeing dead backend during
the brief cliSocket=null window and triggering spurious relaunches.

Fix: debounce the disconnect notification by 5s. In handleCLIClose,
set a timer instead of broadcasting immediately. In handleCLIOpen,
cancel the timer if CLI reconnects within the window. Browsers never
see the disconnection during normal WS cycling.

Also replaces the previous sed-based stale socket guard with a
Python-based multi-line patcher (sed + nix indentation stripping was
fragile). Adds python3 to nativeBuildInputs.

Verified: 5 minutes of clean operation with zero disconnect events,
zero reconnect cycling, and zero spurious relaunches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use full natal chart calculation with birth_time and birth_city (#108)

The astrology graph node was only calling approximate_sun_sign() and
approximate_sun_degree(), ignoring birth_time and birth_city entirely.
Users who entered birth time got no moon sign, rising sign, aspects,
or house placements.

Changes:
- graphs.py: Replace approximate_sun_sign/degree with
  calculate_natal_chart(birth_date, birth_time, birth_city) which
  computes moon sign, rising sign, aspects, and house placements
- orchestrator.py: Enrich downstream coordinators (Healing, Wealth,
  Creative, Perspective) with astrology data including sun_sign,
  moon_sign, and rising_sign
- test_graphs.py: Update mock target from approximate_sun_sign to
  calculate_natal_chart

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
WUHU-0077: Runner protocol v3 — RunnerCommands/RunnerCallbacks (#39)

* WUHU-0077: Runner protocol v3 — RunnerCommands/RunnerCallbacks

Replace the blocking `Runner` actor protocol with short-lived RPCs:
- `RunnerCommands` (server→runner): startBash/waitForBashResult/cancelBash
  plus all file I/O ops (read/write/exists/ls/enumerate/mkdir/find/grep)
- `RunnerCallbacks` (runner→server): bashOutput/bashFinished push callbacks
- `BashCallbackBridge`: actor bridging async bash results via CheckedContinuation
- `InMemoryRunnerCommands`/`InMemoryRunnerCallbacks`: in-process test doubles

Transport layer:
- `MuxRunnerCommandsClient`: actor wrapping MuxSession as RunnerCommands;
  startBash spawns a background Task per bash op (non-blocking handshake)
- `MuxRunnerCommandsServer`: serves RunnerCommands over mux streams
- Bump mux runner protocol version 9 → 10

Bash cancel:
- Remove `BashReaper`; cancel handled directly in the bash tool via
  `withTaskCancellationHandler { } onCancel: { runner.cancelBash(tag:) }`

Updated throughout: RunnerRegistry, MountResolver, CodingAgentTools,
WuhuService, WuhuAgentTools, WuhuMuxRunnerServer, transport connectors.

All tests updated to use new RunnerCommands-based doubles and patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix four issues from runner protocol v3 code review

Address all four review comments from Codex:

1. [P1] Add InMemoryRunner typealias for backward compatibility
2. [P1] Buffer bash results in BashCallbackBridge for fast-completing commands
3. [P2] Clean up activeBash entries after task completion (no leak)
4. [P2] Propagate remote cancel RPC failures instead of swallowing

Also remove gutted stub files (RunnerServerHandler, MuxRunnerClient,
MuxRunnerHandler) that were replaced by their v3 counterparts.

* Add swiftformat pre-commit hook and fix formatting

- Add .githooks/pre-commit that auto-formats staged Swift files
- Set core.hooksPath=.githooks (must be set per-clone; done on template sources)
- Run swiftformat to fix 63 lint errors in files from the v3 protocol change

* Remove stale BashReaper.swift stub

---------

Co-authored-by: Minsheng Liu <minsheng@wuhu.dev>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: avatar persistence + reliable 4/4 image generation

Root cause: Kie.ai-generated ~8MB PNGs exceeded the 5MB Supabase Storage
bucket limit, silently failing persistence. Additionally, Kie.ai's
nano-banana-pro model occasionally drops 1 of 4 parallel tasks.

Persistence fixes (all 3 edge functions):
- Use Uint8Array instead of Blob for Deno → Supabase compatibility
- Increase size limit from 5MB to 10MB (matching bucket config change)
- Add multi-strategy download (3 strategies for R2 signed URL quirks)
- Add magic byte content-type detection (PNG/JPEG/WebP)
- Fix blob.size → bytes.length reference error in persist-avatar-image

Reliability fixes (4/4 images):
- Create 5 Kie.ai tasks instead of 4 (5th as insurance against drops)
- Complete session when 4 of 5 tasks succeed (TARGET_IMAGE_COUNT)
- Extend completion timeout from 90s to 150s
- Accept late callbacks on already-completed sessions
- Add duplicate URL guard in callback handler
- Cap response at 4 images for client

Client updates:
- Increase polling from 45 to 75 attempts (150s match)
- Update progress estimate from 50s to 80s
- Update display text to "45–90 seconds"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add batch delete with checkboxes to admin grants tables

- New selectedIds state (Set<string>) and batchDeleting flag
- toggleSelect / toggleSelectAll helpers
- batchDelete function calls existing PATCH /api/admin/update-grant
- Sticky red batch actions bar appears when any grants are selected
- Select-all checkbox in header of New, Review, and Scraped grants tables
- Per-row checkboxes with red row highlight when selected
- Selection clears automatically when switching filter tabs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor soft-delete endpoints from DELETE to PATCH (#88)

Soft-delete operations (setting deletedAt timestamp) are semantically
partial updates, not true deletions. Per RFC 7231, DELETE request body
has no defined semantics and may be stripped by infrastructure.

Changed projects, structures, and findings soft-delete endpoints to use
PATCH instead of DELETE. Added patch method to SnagNetworkHttpClient
interface and implementation for frontend API clients.

Closes #16

https://claude.ai/code/session_01GY2KjXhgBBSnbaRHVW9X4f

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

and updates test counts in mvp-progress.md.

Fix TypeScript error: coerce undefined to null in FTP/CSS fallback chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 26 failure-path bugs across 10 files + 2 updated tests

Systematic 5-sweep audit of every failure path: exception handlers,
unchecked write returns, retry idempotency, pipeline failure gating,
and premature success messages. Key fixes: _committed_session_id
persisted to checkpoint (prevents duplicate data on crash recovery),
claims dedup before insert on retry, batch_commit/ops success requires
actual storage not just session_id, success banners gated on committed
state, rollback write order fixed to MongoDB-first, 6 silent except:pass
blocks now log, and consistency API errors distinguished from "all clear".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement full SSE event stream proxy

Implements a complete SSE (Server-Sent Events) proxy for the Approval
system migration with the following components:

New Modules:
- sse_proxy/__init__.py: Package exports
- sse_proxy/buffer.py: EventBufferManager with thread-safe buffering
- sse_proxy/connection.py: SSEConnectionManager with auto-reconnection
- sse_proxy/transform.py: EventTransformer for format conversion
- sse_proxy/proxy.py: SSEProxy main coordinator

Features:
- Persistent SSE connection to VPS Agent Web
- Exponential backoff reconnection (1s, 2s, 4s... max 30s)
- Thread-safe event buffering with 5-min history
- Event format transformation (remote ↔ local)
- Client subscription management
- Automatic cleanup of stale clients

Updates:
- adapter.py: REMOTE_ONLY mode now uses SSE proxy
- __init__.py: Export SSE proxy classes

Resolves task #24.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: client-driven server auto-update with POST /admin/update

On connect, the client compares its version against the server's
/health version. If the client is newer, it POSTs to /admin/update
which runs `npm install -g @schuttdev/gigai@latest`, responds with
success, then restarts the server process. Includes semver-aware
comparison handling prereleases. Version bumped to 0.2.2.
